mainmatter.p: Prove that the total category of \overline { B } is the arrow category B ^{ \to }, and the projection is the domain functor.
Dually to the fundamental self-indexing, every category B can also be displayed over itself via its coslices B ^{ \setminus x }.
Let B be a category. Define the displayed category \overline { B } over B as follows:

ol.li: For x \in B, define \overline { B } _{ x } as the collection of pairs ( \bar {x} \in B,p _{ x } :x \to \bar {x}).
For f : x \to y \in B, define \overline { B } _{ f } to be the collection of commuting squares in the following configuration:

mainmatter.p: Prove that \overline { B } is a cocartesian fibration if and only if B has pushouts.
Prove that \overline { B } is a cartesian fibration for any category B.

frontmatter.title: The total category and its projection

mainmatter.p: Note that any displayed category E over B can be viewed as an undisplayed category \widetilde { E } equipped with a projection functor p _{ E } : \widetilde { E } \to B; in this case \widetilde { E } is called the total category of E.

ol.li: An object of \widetilde { E } is given by a pair (x, \bar {x}) where x \in B and \bar {x} \in E _{ x }.

A morphism { (x, \bar {x}) } \xrightarrow {{ }}{ (y, \bar {y}) } in \widetilde { E } is given by a pair (f, \bar {f}) where { x } \xrightarrow {{ f }}{ y } and { \bar {x} } \xrightarrow [ f ]{ \bar {f} }{ \bar {y} } : \bar {x}.

mainmatter.p: The construction of the total category of a displayed category is called the Grothendieck construction.
Prove that the total category \widetilde { \underline { B } } of the fundamental self-indexing is the arrow category B^{ \to }, and the projection is the codomain functor.
